description: MCP Client — connects an MCP-compatible client (VS Code, Claude Desktop, etc.) to this workspace via a scoped API key

title.description: Human-readable connection title shown on the artifact

notes.description: Optional setup notes for the client connection

workspace_id.description: Workspace the generated API key is scoped to

client.description: Client-facing metadata used by the artifact UI

name.description: Client label, for example VS Code Copilot or Claude Desktop

kind.description: Optional client family hint for future UX

connection.description: Derived MCP endpoint details displayed to the user

auth_header.description: Header name the client should send, usually Authorization

api_key.description: Stored API key metadata. The raw secret is never stored on the artifact.

id.description: API key id used for refresh, rotation, and revocation

preview.description: Non-secret display hint for the one-time key that was shown to the user

is_active.description: Whether the referenced API key is still active

scopes.description: Granted scopes for the MCP client connection

resource_filters.description: Resource scoping applied to the key, typically limited to one workspace

created_time.description: Creation timestamp returned by the API keys service

last_used_at.description: Last observed use of the key, when available

client_id.description: Optional OAuth client ID for authorization code flow. Set to the artifact ID when redirect_uris are registered. Used by VS Code, Claude Desktop, and other OAuth-capable MCP clients.

redirect_uris.description: Optional list of allowed OAuth redirect URIs (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:33418, https://vscode.dev/redirect). Required only for OAuth authorization code flow — not needed for API key (Bearer token) access.

allowed_oauth_scopes.description: OAuth scopes this client is permitted to receive via the authorization code flow. Used by the token endpoint to restrict issued scopes to a safe subset (e.g. ["read", "write"]). Defaults to ["read"] if absent. Never grants roles, email, name, or picture — those are PII reserved for the platform client.

fields.label: MCP Server URL
fields.hint: Paste this into your MCP client as the server URL

fields.label: API Key
fields.hint: Shown once — copy it now. Use Rotate to generate a new key.

fields.label: OAuth allowed scopes
fields.hint: Scopes this client can request via OAuth authorization code flow. Leave empty to default to [read].
